chinese_text: 不尚賢，使民不爭；不貴難得之貨，
translation_text: Not to value and employ men of superior ability is the way to keep the people from rivalry among themselves;
translation_text_raw: Not to value and employ men of superior ability is the way to keep the people from rivalry among themselves;

translation_text: not to prize articles which are difficult to procure is the way to keep them from becoming thieves;
translation_text_raw: not to prize articles which are difficult to procure is the way to keep them from becoming thieves;

chinese_text: 使民心不亂。是以聖人之治，
translation_text: not to show them what is likely to excite their desires is the way to keep their minds from disorder.
translation_text_raw: not to show them what is likely to excite their desires is the way to keep their minds from disorder.

chinese_text: 虛其心，實其腹，弱其志，強其骨。
translation_text: Therefore the sage, in the exercise of his government, empties their minds, fills their bellies, weakens their wills, and strengthens their bones.
translation_text_raw: Therefore the sage, in the exercise of his government, empties their minds, fills their bellies, weakens their wills, and strengthens their bones.

chinese_text: 常使民無知無欲。使夫智者不敢為也。
translation_text: He constantly (tries to) keep them without knowledge and without desire, and where there are those who have knowledge, to keep them from presuming to act (on it).
translation_text_raw: He constantly (tries to) keep them without knowledge and without desire, and where there are those who have knowledge, to keep them from presuming to act (on it).

translation_text: When there is this abstinence from action, good order is universal.
translation_text_raw: When there is this abstinence from action, good order is universal.
